_comment2: ACS Deploy-time policy: block containers running as root (UID 0)
_comment3: Scoped to application namespaces only (not tools or infra namespaces)

policies.name: DevSecOps - Block Root User Containers (Deploy)
policies.description: Prevents deployment of containers that run as root (UID 0) in application namespaces. Containers must use a non-root user (e.g., USER 1001 in Dockerfile).
policies.rationale: Running containers as root violates the principle of least privilege and expands the attack surface. A container escape from a root process grants host-level access.
policies.remediation: Set USER 1001 (or another non-root UID) in the Dockerfile. Ensure the application does not require root privileges. The SampleAPI Dockerfile already sets USER 1001.
